captions.best: The best programmer the world has ever seen

programming.title: Programming
programming.content: C#, C++, GLSL, HTML, TS, SCSS, Bash and Nix

other.title: Other
other.content: Deep understanding of Linux (I love LVM [Btrfs too!])

paragraph1.line1: Look, I'm not here to tell a 5 page story but I do think that I should tell a bit more about myself.

paragraph2.line1: I was raised in Hessen, Germany, am 17 years old (as of writing this)
paragraph2.line4: I manage the entire home network all by myself

paragraph3.line1: When I was in 7th grade my brother introduced me to python. I hacked together some GUI apps and never touched Python again (he even installed Anaconda for me...)
paragraph3.line2: In 7th grade I had an internship at a software company called Somentec. There I was confronted with Dataflex, the loveliest language.
paragraph3.line3: I had a lot of fun there and learned a very useful skill, debugging (yay!)
paragraph3.line4: An employee there also told me to try out Scratch. I was sceptical at first, and I ended up creating a lot of projects (and had a lot of fun of course!)

paragraph4.line1: Then a long period of nothing came.

paragraph5.line1: In 2019 I decided to pick up C#, went to learn Unity to ditch it in 2020.
paragraph5.line2: Unfortunately, in Q2 2020 my father's brother died to cancer.
paragraph5.line3: My father had a lot of unsorted images of his brother on his harddrives so I proposed that I would write a small app which would collect and sort said images (a big project for me at the time).
paragraph5.line4: And thus my first project, FileFinderGit was born!
paragraph5.line5: I learned how to use libraries and rapidly learned C# while rewriting said app.
paragraph5.line6: In Q1 2020 I also fully switched to Linux and ditched Windows (losing some savegames in the process, RIP)

paragraph6.line1: At one point I have gotten so proficient with C# that I decided to try out other languages.
paragraph6.line3: Then came computer science class, which taught me mainly HTML and CSS.
paragraph6.line4: As of now we are learning Java (It's a downgrade compared to C#).
paragraph6.line5: I work on my own little game engine and enjoy playing osu! (and of course watching anime).

paragraph7.line1: To this day I think of the internship and of the people that supported me on my journey. I am extremely grateful for the support I've been recieving from my family and friends (both IRL and online)
paragraph7.line2: May my father's brother rest in piece. I wish his family the best.

repos.compat: This part looks best if your browser supports css:backdrop-filter (which most do). However, on firefox this is disabled by default.
